headline: Retail investor buying holds strong: Why these names are winning
summary: Amid heightened geopolitical volatility, retail investors have been rotating back into defense stock and tried-and-true names, like Nvidia (NVDA) and Microsoft (MSFT). Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan join Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi to discuss recent retail investing trends. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid.

headline: 4 Winning Space Stocks To Buy Before The Artemis Launch
summary: NASA’s Artemis II mission is targeting an April 2026 launch window, and prediction markets currently place the odds of liftoff by April 30 at roughly 54.5%. The rocket was rolled back to the Vehicle Assembly Building in late February for repairs, which pushed the timeline past earlier projections, but the program remains very much alive. ... 4 Winning Space Stocks To Buy Before The Artemis Launch

headline: Had You Invested $1,000 in Northrop Grumman or Lockheed Martin a Decade Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now
summary: On March 2, 2026, as news broke of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear and military sites during Operation Epic Fury, defense stocks moved opposite to nearly everything else. Northrop Grumman (NYSE: NOC) jumped 4.6% in premarket trading, while Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) rose more than 3%, even as the broader S&P 500 sold ... Had You Invested $1,000 in Northrop Grumman or Lockheed Martin a Decade Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now

headline: U.S. Defense Manufacturers Face A Rare Earth Supply Squeeze - OilPrice.com Market Commentary
summary: According to sources, the Pentagon will prohibit the use of rare earth magnet materials originating from China in U.S. military platforms beginning in 2027. This mandate will have broad implications across the American defense industrial base, requiring manufacturers to verify the origin of the rare earth metals used in their systems, tracing them back to the earliest stages of the processing chain. Companies mentioned in this release include: REalloys Inc. (ALOY), Olin Corporation (NYSE: OLN),

content.summary: According to sources, the Pentagon will prohibit the use of rare earth magnet materials originating from China in U.S. military platforms beginning in 2027. headline: Northrop Grumman Secures a $225M Contract to Aid E-130J Program
summary: NOC wins a $225.1M Naval Air Systems Command contract to develop training materials for the E-130J program, boosting its position in the growing military training market.

content.summary: NOC wins a $225.1M Naval Air Systems Command contract to develop training materials for the E-130J program, boosting its position in the growing military training market.

headline: Embraer's Fourth-Quarter Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
summary: EMBJ beats Q4 estimates with $2.65 billion in revenues and strong jet deliveries as defense, executive aviation and services growth help lift the backlog to $31.6 billion.

content.title: Embraer's Fourth-Quarter Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
content.summary: EMBJ beats Q4 estimates with $2.65 billion in revenues and strong jet deliveries as defense, executive aviation and services growth help lift the backlog to $31.6 billion.

summary: By Mike Stone WASHINGTON, March 6 (Reuters) - U.S.

headline: Trump Says Defense Firms to Quadruple Output of Some Weapons
summary: “We just concluded a very good meeting with the largest U.S. Defense Manufacturing Companies where we discussed Production and Production Schedules,” Trump said in a social media post on Friday.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity.”

content.title: Trump Says Defense Firms to Quadruple Output of Some Weapons

headline: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.

content.title: Trump Says Weapons Makers Agree to Expand Production
content.summary: President Trump said America’s largest weapons makers have agreed to raise production of military munitions following “a very good meeting” at the White House on Friday.  Executives from BAE Systems, Boeing, Honeywell Aerospace, L3Harris Technologies, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and RTX attended Friday’s meeting, Trump said.  “They have agreed to quadruple Production of the ‘Exquisite Class’ Weaponry in that we want to reach, as rapidly as possible, the highest levels of quantity,” Trump wrote in a social media post.

headline: Trump to Quadruple Missile Production. What Global Rearmament Means for Defense Stocks.
summary: Rising global military spending—from the U.S., Europe, and China—could fuel growth for defense contractors and reshape manufacturing and employment in the years ahead.

content.summary: Rising global military spending—from the U.S., Europe, and China—could fuel growth for defense contractors and reshape manufacturing and employment in the years ahead.

headline: Defense-tech stocks are the hot trade as Iran conflict widens
summary: In a week when conflict in Iran sent the U.S. equity market into a tailspin, technology stocks tied to cybersecurity and artificial intelligence have proved more resilient than sectors investors have typically treated as safe havens.

content.title: Defense-tech stocks are the hot trade as Iran conflict widens
content.summary: In a week when conflict in Iran sent the U.S. equity market into a tailspin, technology stocks tied to cybersecurity and artificial intelligence have proved more resilient than sectors investors have typically treated as safe havens.
